description_long: This is a small, single-engine airplane with a high-wing configuration and a tricycle landing gear. The fuselage is streamlined and painted in a combination of blue and silver, with roundels on the wings and a visible insignia on the tail. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage and is enclosed with a transparent canopy, likely seating one or two occupants. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the tail features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear consists of two main wheels under the wings and a nose wheel, all of which appear fixed. The overall design suggests a light utility or trainer aircraft, suitable for short takeoff and landing operations.
description: A small, high-wing, single-propeller airplane with blue and silver coloring, tricycle landing gear, and an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the propeller, nose wheel, and the symmetrical high-wing layout.
description_view_1: Oblique rear-left view highlighting the tail insignia, wing roundel, and the fixed main landing gear.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, wing roundels, and the T-shaped tail structure.
description_view_3: Oblique rear-right view showing the tail section, horizontal stabilizers, and the right main landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, all fixed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, likely single or dual seat.
